qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated Midjourney image proxy exposes private task images
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes an authentication bypass in GET /mj/image/:id caused by Gin route registration order (router/relay-router.go:189), where the route is registered before TokenAuth middleware is applied. The advisory describes a completely different vulnerability: SQL LIKE wildcard injection in /api/token/search via model/token.go:70, where user-supplied keyword and token parameters are directly concatenated into LIKE clauses without escaping % and _ wildcards. The root cause, vulnerable code, endpoint, and vulnerability class (authentication bypass vs SQL injection/DoS) are all different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes SQL LIKE wildcard injection in /api/token/search via model/token.go:70, where user-supplied keyword and token parameters are concatenated into LIKE clauses without escaping wildcard characters, enabling DoS through resource exhaustion. The sole qualifying finding (unauthenticated-mj-image-proxy) describes an authentication bypass in GET /mj/image/:id caused by Gin route registration order, which is a completely different endpoint, file, root cause, and vulnerability class.
evidence: Advisory vulnerable code: model/token.go:70 — WHERE clauses use "%"+keyword+"%" and "%"+token+"%" without escaping SQL LIKE wildcards.
Advisory endpoint: /api/token/search with keyword and token query parameters.
Finding vulnerable code: router/relay-router.go:189 — GET /mj/image/:id registered before TokenAuth() middleware at line 190.
Finding endpoint: GET /mj/image/:id — completely different from /api/token/search.
No finding references model/token.go, the token search endpoint, or SQL LIKE wildcard escaping.
